text: Life is not a problem to be solved but a mystery to be lived. The moment you stop trying to control it and simply become aware of what is, the ordinary transforms into the sacred. Meditation is not a technique but a return to your natural state of consciousness, where joy, love, and wisdom arise not from effort but from total acceptance of the here and now.

It teaches that consciousness is not an app to download but a fire already burning within, waiting to be noticed. As AI reshapes work, the human frontier becomes inner awareness, emotional authenticity, and the courage to live without a script.

By welcoming anger, sadness, fear, and even so-called negative moods as natural weather, the shadow is brought into the light of awareness where it dissolves.

text: Osho Everyday Meditations is a year-long invitation to drop the struggle and return to the natural state of consciousness that society has buried under conditioning, guilt, and the obsession with becoming. Across three hundred and sixty-five entries, Osho dismantles the illusion that life is a problem to be solved, revealing instead that joy, love, and wisdom arise spontaneously when you stop interfering with yourself. He speaks of meditation not as a technique but as the simple art of being here now, of watching without judgment, and of allowing the mind to empty so that being can overflow. Whether addressing anger or orgasm, work or aloneness, trust or death, each meditation points to the same truth: you are already whole, already free, and already home. The only thing missing is the courage to accept it. For anyone feeling lost in the noise of the modern world, this book is a quiet campfire reminding you that the light you seek is the very light you are.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Short Summary
metadata.keywords: meditation, consciousness, presence, spontaneity, love, acceptance, ego death, joy

text: Meditation is not a method to be learned but a light to be recognized. You already carry within you a small flame of consciousness, no larger than a single candle in a vast dark house, yet that flame is enough to begin with. The work is simply to grow it until every corner of your inner world is bright. When that happens, the ordinary stone becomes a diamond, the mundane walk becomes a pilgrimage, and the stranger becomes a revelation. You have been waiting for the world to change, but the world changes only when the seer changes. If you are dark inside, the whole of existence appears dark. If you are illuminated, the whole of existence joins in that illumination. This is why no mantra and no rosary can substitute for awareness, because they are borrowed lights that flicker and fade. The light that is your own consciousness never fades. It only waits for you to stop looking elsewhere and notice that it has been burning all along, patient and eternal, ready to flood the house with a radiance that turns every moment into a miracle.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Consciousness is an inner light that transforms all of existence

text: There is a strange law that governs the soul of work. When you begin something new, your whole being pours into it, and the result carries a freshness that no expert can manufacture. But as you become skilled, something else happens. You start repeating what you know, and repetition slowly replaces invention. The expert has too much at stake to risk the unknown, because every new discovery threatens the old skill. This is why all great breakthroughs come from amateurs, from those who are foolish enough not to know what cannot be done. The lesson is not to abandon skill but to refuse to settle inside it. Whenever your work begins to feel stale, become an amateur again. Add something bizarre, delete something safe, and let the pattern break. It takes guts to return to ignorance, but that return is the only way life remains alive. Skill is a beautiful servant and a terrible master. Let it serve you, then set it aside, and step into the next thing as if you have never walked before.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Fresh creativity lives only before skill hardens into repetition

text: The world has been arranged upside down. You have been taught that the individual exists for society, that you must fit the mold even when the mold is insane, and that your nature is dangerous while social rules are safe. But society is only an institution, a machine without a soul, while you are the conscious being who breathes and feels. Nature demands one thing, and society often demands the opposite, and this conflict is the root of your anxiety. Whenever you find yourself torn between what feels true in your body and what is expected by the crowd, choose nature whatever the cost. The cost of choosing society is that you slowly become a ghost in your own life, performing roles that never included your heart. The cost of choosing nature is that you may be misunderstood, but you will never be lost. You will remain real, and reality is the only soil in which consciousness can grow. The garden was lost the moment society replaced instinct with obedience.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Whenever society and instinct conflict, instinct is the true compass

text: Love that makes demands is not love at all. It is a transaction dressed in beautiful language, and transactions always create ledgers of debt and resentment. True love is unmotivated. It does not ask what it will receive, nor does it keep score of what it has given. It is like a cloud that rains on mountains and rocks alike, never checking whether the earth is worthy. When you love in this way, something mysterious happens. The world becomes an echoing place. What you throw out returns, not because you planned it, but because that is the simple law of existence. Throw anger, and anger finds you. Throw love, and love surrounds you. This is not a strategy for getting love back. It is a description of how reality works when you stop interfering with it. The moment you love for its own sake, without motive and without boundary, you become a source rather than a beggar. You are no longer waiting to be filled. You are overflowing, and the overflow is the only wealth that never runs dry.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Love given without motive returns upon the giver multiplied

text: How many times have you fought with someone you love, spoken words that cut, and then an hour later understood that none of it was necessary? This is the tragedy of retrospective wisdom. It gives you the feeling that you have grown, but it is a trick of the ego because the harm has already been done. Real wisdom is not the understanding that comes after the storm. It is the awareness that arises while the storm is still happening. If you can see your own nonsense in the very moment you are creating it, you cannot continue it. One never goes against one's own awareness. The other person is never truly responsible for your reaction. The problem is always something boiling inside you, and the one you love is simply the nearest target. Make this a permanent state of alertness. The moment you start finding something wrong with the other, catch yourself red-handed and drop it right there. Ask to be excused if you must, but do not let the moment pass in blindness.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Understanding arrives too late when it comes after the act

text: You do not own your breath. You do not own the sunrise. You do not own the fact that your heart continues beating while you sleep. These are gifts, and the moment you recognize them as gifts rather than entitlements, a revolution begins inside you. Start feeling grateful for small things, not only for the grand events. Be thankful for the sheer fact of breathing, for the warmth of water on your hands, for the sound of silence in the night. Gratitude is not a polite gesture. It is a style of being, a climate in which misery cannot survive. Misery is hooked to the complaining mind like a parasite to its host. The more you complain, the more misery finds reasons to stay. The more you give thanks, the more the parasite starves. This is one of the most important secrets ever discovered, and it costs nothing. You do not need to change your circumstances. You only need to change the lens through which you see them, from grievance to gift.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Thankfulness for small things dissolves the complaining mind
metadata.keywords: gratitude, acceptance, joy, small things, presence

text: Why do you wait for a reason to laugh? Life as it is should be reason enough. It is so absurd, so ridiculous, so beautifully chaotic that if you really look at it, laughter is the only sane response. But people have become misers of laughter. They laugh as if they are fulfilling a duty, as if they are obliging the world with a polite noise. Real laughter is fun. It is wild. It bursts out of you without an invitation, and it changes the chemistry of your entire body. The trees are laughing in the wind, the river is laughing over the stones, and the child is laughing at a pebble. Only the adult stands there grim, collecting reasons. Drop the miserliness. Look into anything deeply enough and you will find the joke. Your seriousness is not strength. It is a defense against the vulnerability of joy. Laughter is the real religion. Everything else is just a rehearsal.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Life is absurd enough to laugh without waiting for a reason

text: You are sitting with a friend and suddenly your whole being wants to be silent. Right in the middle of a sentence, the desire comes to stop. So stop. Do not complete the sentence just because etiquette demands it. Do not judge yourself for becoming silent. Do not tell yourself that it is rude or strange. In deep acceptance, everything becomes a blessing. Your totality wanted silence, and wherever your totality goes, you must follow because there is no other goal. For a few days the world around you may be puzzled. They may think you are odd. But by and by they will understand, and more importantly, you will understand yourself. The relaxation that comes from following your inner current without self-condemnation is tremendous. It is the relaxation of a person who is no longer fighting themselves. You become a shadow to your own wholeness, and that shadow is lighter than any burden you have ever carried.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Following your totality without self-condemnation brings relaxation

text: You spend your life guarding things that can be stolen, and in that guarding you miss everything that cannot. The real robbers are not outside. They are doubt, suspicion, and fear, and they destroy your very possibility of celebration. Because of fear you cannot love fully. Because of suspicion you cannot open your heart. Because of doubt you cannot surrender to the unknown. You become cautious, and caution is just fear wearing a mask of intelligence. Be conscious, but never be cautious. Consciousness is aware and free. Caution is afraid and closed. The cautious person treads the same path back and forth like a freight train on a fixed track, never exploring new lands, never risking the wrong turn. But you have nothing to lose that matters. All that can be taken from you is not worth keeping. What is worth keeping cannot be stolen. So while you are here, celebrate the earth. While this moment lasts, enjoy it to the very core.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Doubt, suspicion, and fear steal life while you guard empty possessions

text: The critical mind is a brilliant tool when you are dissecting a problem, building a machine, or testing a hypothesis. But it is an absolute barrier when you turn it toward your own interiority. With the objective world, doubt is the method. Without doubt there is no science. But with the subjective world, doubt becomes a wall. The same mind that discovers electrons cannot discover the self, because the self is not an object to be analyzed. It is the very presence that is doing the analyzing. This is why the conflict between science and religion was never really about institutions. It was a conflict between two dimensions of being, the outer and the inner, each requiring a different quality of attention. Learn to use your critical mind as a servant, not as a master. When you are working outwardly, sharpen it. When you are turning inward, put it aside. The ability to shift between these two modes is the beginning of a total intelligence that neither the laboratory nor the monastery alone can provide.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Analysis serves objects but blocks entry into subjectivity
metadata.keywords: critical mind, awareness, science, consciousness, subjectivity

text: There are rare moments when the ego simply vanishes. In deep orgasm, in total love, in sudden drunkenness, your history recedes and disappears. You do not know who you are. You have no name, no story, no autobiography. For a few seconds you are utterly here, and that utter hereness is the source of all refreshment, all rejuvenation, all silence. Then the past rushes back in, the ego reclaims its throne, and you wonder where the magic went. The magic did not go anywhere. You simply left it. The ego is not a reality. It is your history, and history is always dead. To keep it alive requires constant effort, constant vigilance, constant fear that someone will see through it. But if you allow those moments of total presence to happen without running from them, you will start to see that the ego is the enemy, not because it is evil, but because it is false. And what is false should be the easiest thing to let die.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: In total drunkenness the past disappears and only presence remains

text: Humanity has been poisoned by guilt for centuries. You were told that certain thoughts make you a sinner and certain actions make you a saint, and you believed it. You shaped yourself to fit the expectations of churches and crowds, and in doing so you buried your naturalness under layers of shame. But guilt is not natural. It is manufactured, and what is manufactured can be unmade. The moment you stop calling yourself a sinner for being human, a chain reaction begins. Feeling less guilty, you immediately feel happier. Feeling happier, you feel less conflicted. Feeling less conflicted, you feel harmonious. Feeling harmonious, a certain grace surrounds you. Each state triggers the next, and the chain moves toward a light that no institution can control. Move into a new consciousness where you unguilt yourself completely. Do not ask permission. Do not wait for forgiveness. Simply drop the weight, and you will be surprised how many other things drop with it.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Dropping manufactured guilt sets off a reaction toward joy

text: You are young in proportion to your flexibility, not to the number of years you have lived. Watch a small child. The body is soft, tender, and adaptable. As people grow older they become tight, hard, and fixed, not because age demands it but because fear demands it. When you are afraid, you shrink. You hide in your shell. You pull back from love, from expression, from meditation, and by and by the elasticity of your energy is lost. You become a stagnant pool instead of a flowing river. But fear has a natural use. When the house is on fire, you should shrink and escape. The art is to remain capable of both expansion and contraction, to go out and to come in, to breathe deeply and to hold the breath when needed. This is flexibility, and it is the secret of remaining alive until your very last day. Sometimes even anger is good because at least it moves your energy. Between fear and anger, choose anger. But better than both is the playful expansion of a person who trusts life enough to stay open.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Remaining soft and elastic keeps the energy flowing like a river

text: Grace is not something you achieve. It is the aura that surrounds total acceptance. When you move spontaneously, each moment decides for itself how it will be. This moment does not decide for the next. You remain open-ended, without plan, without pattern, without a manager inside. Today is enough. Do not design tomorrow, because tomorrow will arrive fresh and innocent on its own, opening itself without your manipulation. Watch a flower opening in the morning. There is no effort at all. It simply moves according to nature. Watch a cat awakening, effortlessly, with tremendous grace surrounding it. The whole of nature is full of grace, but you have lost the capacity because of your divisions. You have divided today from tomorrow, action from result, self from world. Stop fighting with existence. Surrender. Let the moment decide. This is what is meant by let-go, and everything that is beautiful happens out of this alone.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Moving without plan allows each moment to decide its own shape

text: There is an ordinary fear that has a name. You are afraid of death, of disease, of old age. These are common fears, garden variety, and the mind knows how to handle them because they have explanations. But there is a special fear that comes without an object. It hovers around you for no reason at all, and that makes it truly terrifying. If you can find a reason, the mind is satisfied. It can explain the fear away, and explanation feels like control. But when no reason can be found, the mind panics. This is the fear every seeker must pass through. It is the sign that you are on the verge of something unknown, something that your old maps cannot chart. Do not ask why. Do not demand an explanation. Something unknown is hovering around you, as it hovers around everyone who dares to seek. I am not here to give you explanations but to push you into the experience itself. The more you experience, the richer you become. The unknown is not your enemy. It is the doorway you have been looking for.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Objectless fear signals you are on the verge of the unknown

text: The body is one, but you have been taught to divide it. The diaphragm is the place where the split occurs. Everything below it has been called lower, animal, sinful. Everything above it has been called higher, spiritual, pure. This bifurcation is not natural. It is a dangerous lie that makes you reject half of your own existence. Whatever you exclude from your life does not disappear. It goes into the stomach, it collects near the diaphragm, and it waits. Medical researchers now say that cancer is nothing but too much stress inside, and cancer exists most in repressed societies. In a primitive society where the whole body is accepted, where nothing is lower and nothing is higher, the energy flows as one. The day your control explodes, you will feel reborn. The divided body will reconnect, and you will discover that the body was never your enemy. It was your home, and you were the one who locked half of it in the basement.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Repression splits the body at the diaphragm and invites disease

text: When I speak of ignorance, I do not mean the absence of knowledge. I mean something fundamental, present, and positive. It is the very nature of existence to remain mysterious, and that is why it is so beautiful. All knowledge is superfluous. It creates the illusion that we know, but we do not know. You can live with someone your whole life and think you know them, and you do not know them. You can give birth to a child and think you know the child, and you do not know the child. We have played a game of naming things, categorizing things, but life remains a mystery. Ignorance is so profound that it cannot be destroyed. Once you understand this, you can rest in it. There is nowhere to go, nothing to be known, because nothing can be known. Ignorance is ultimate, and in that ultimate not-knowing, a tremendous relaxation happens.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Not-knowing is the final rest because mystery cannot be solved

text: Out of one hundred people suffering from anger, about fifty are suffering from too much creative energy that they have not been able to use. Their problem is not anger at all, but they will go on thinking it is anger their whole life. Once a problem is diagnosed rightly, half of it is already solved. Put your energies into creativity. Forget about anger as a problem and ignore it. Channel your energy toward something you love. Pour yourself into painting, into music, into dance, into anything that makes you feel alive. Shift from anger to creativity and immediately you will see a great change arising in you. Tomorrow the same things will not feel like excuses for rage because now your energy is moving, it is enjoying itself, it is dancing. Who cares about small irritations when you are creating something beautiful? The energy that was poisoning you becomes your medicine.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Redirecting unused creative energy dissolves the excuse for rage

text: Whatever you do, do it as totally as possible. If you enjoy walking, walk. If suddenly you realize you no longer have the urge to move, sit down immediately. Not even a single step should be taken against your totality. Whatever happens, accept and enjoy it, and do not force anything. If you feel like talking, talk. If you feel like being silent, be silent. Just move with the feeling. The whole of humanity has become almost schizophrenic because we have been taught to force things. The part that wants to laugh and the part that does not allow you to laugh become separate, and then you are divided. You create a top dog and an underdog, and the conflict can become bigger and bigger. The problem is how to bridge that rift, and the answer is simple. Do not force. In Zen they have a beautiful saying. Sitting, just sit. Walking, just walk. Above all, do not wobble.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Acting totally with the moment prevents inner division

text: Why do we hold back in love? There is a fear that if we give all, then we have nothing else to give, so we give only in parts. We keep the carrot dangling. We want to remain mysterious. But when you do not allow the other to enter into your whole being, it is because of the fear that once the other knows you totally they may become disinterested. You keep a few corners aloof so that the other goes on wondering what more you have. And the other is doing exactly the same thing. There is an animal understanding behind this. Once the mystery is known, the thing is finished. We love the unknown. When it is mapped and measured, the adventuring mind starts thinking of other pastures. But when you are truly independent and surrendered to the god of love, you can open yourself totally. In that very opening you become one. When two people are open, they are no longer two. When the walls disappear, the room is one, and that is where fulfillment lives.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Keeping corners hidden kills the mystery that sustains desire
metadata.keywords: love, trust, holding back, mystery, vulnerability

text: We are separate only on the surface. Deep down we are not separate at all. Only the visible part is separate. The invisible part is still one. When you relax and become silent, when you become more humble, more childlike, more innocent, it will be difficult at first to see whether your thoughts are yours or whether they are entering you from the outside. But they are coming from nowhere. They are coming from the deepest core of your being, and that is the core of everybody else. A really original thought carries nobody's signature. It is simply there, out of the collective, out of the universal, out of the one mind. When the individual mind relaxes, the universal mind starts overflooding you. So be like a child. Let your eyes be full of wonder. Lose your moorings. The very idea that you know prevents you from knowing. The very idea that you are ignorant makes you open, available, and ready to receive the whole ocean.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Innocence opens the floodgates of the collective consciousness

text: Do not think that love is eternal. It is very fragile, as fragile as a rose. In the morning it is there, and by the evening it may be gone. Any small thing can destroy it. The higher a thing is, the more fragile it is. A rock will remain, but the flower will be gone. Love is very delicate. One has to be very careful and cautious about it. You can do such harm that the other becomes closed and defensive. If you fight too much, your partner will start escaping. They will become more and more cold, more and more closed, so that they are no longer vulnerable to your attack. Then you will attack them more because you resist that coldness, and this becomes a vicious circle. Lovers drift apart, and they think the other was responsible. But no lover has ever betrayed anybody. It is only ignorance that kills love. Both wanted to be together, but both were ignorant, and their ignorance played tricks on them.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Love is a rose that perishes when handled with carelessness

text: Meditation means to be oneself, and love means to share one's being with somebody else. Meditation gives you the treasure, and love helps you to share it. These are the two most basic things, and all else is nonessential. There is an old story about three travelers who go to Rome. The first says he will stay three months, and the Pope says he will see much. The second says six weeks, and the Pope says he will see more. The third says two weeks, and the Pope says he is fortunate because he will see everything. The travelers were puzzled, but they did not understand the mechanism of the mind. If you had a thousand years, you would miss many things because you would postpone them. But because life is short, you cannot afford to postpone. Yet people do postpone, and at their own cost. If you had only one day left, you would forget all unnecessary things. You would love and pray and meditate, because only twenty-four hours remain. The real things, the essential things, you would not postpone.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Meditation and love are the only necessities; all else is decoration

text: Never ask anybody what is right and what is wrong. Life is an experiment to find out. Each individual has to be conscious, alert, and watchful, and experiment with life to discover what is good for them. Whatever gives you peace, whatever makes you blissful, whatever brings you closer to existence and its harmony is good. Whatever creates conflict, misery, and pain is wrong. Nobody else can decide this for you because every individual has their own world and their own sensitivity. We are unique, so formulas will not work. The whole world is proof of this. Sometimes you may do what is wrong, but that will give you the experience of it. Sometimes you may do something good, and you will be immensely benefited. The rewards are not beyond this life. They are here and now. Each action brings its result immediately. Just be alert and watch. Mature people are those who have watched and found for themselves what is right and what is wrong. By finding it for themselves, they have tremendous authority. The whole world may say something else, and it makes no difference to them. They have their own experience to go by, and that is enough.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Your own peace and bliss are the only valid measures of right

text: There are no outside causes of happiness or unhappiness. These things are just excuses. By and by we come to realize that it is something inside us that goes on changing, and it has nothing to do with the outside. How you feel is a wheel that keeps moving inside you. Just watch it, and it is very beautiful because in being aware of it, something has been attained. Now you understand that you are free from outside excuses. Nothing has happened on the outside, and yet your mood has changed within minutes from happiness to unhappiness, or the other way around. This means that happiness and unhappiness are your moods and do not depend on anything external. This is one of the most basic things to realize, because then much can be done. Your moods depend on your unawareness. So just watch and become aware. If happiness is there, watch it and do not become identified with it. When unhappiness is there, again just watch. It is like morning and evening. You watch the rising sun, and you watch the darkness descend. Both are watched, and neither is owned.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Happiness and unhappiness are moods inside you, not results outside

text: Play, but play knowingly. Play your roles, whatever they are, and do not repress them. Play them as perfectly as possible, but stay fully alert. Enjoy it, and others will also enjoy it. When a person plays a role, there is some reason in it. That role has some significance. If the game is played perfectly, something from the unconscious will disappear and evaporate, and you will be freed from a burden. For example, if you want to play like a child, that means something in your childhood remained incomplete. You could not be a child as you wanted to be. Somebody stopped you. People made you more serious and forced you to appear more adult than you were. Something has remained incomplete, and that incompletion demands to be finished. It will continue to haunt you. So finish it. Nothing is wrong in it. You could not be a child back then. Now you can be. Once you can be totally in it, you will see that it has disappeared and will never come again. The role played consciously completes the past.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Playing a role knowingly completes what the past left unfinished

text: Do not use the words happiness and unhappiness, because these words carry judgments. Simply watch without judging. Just say this is mood A and this is mood B. A mood has gone, and now B mood is here, and you are simply a watcher. Suddenly you will realize that when you call happiness by its name, it is not so happy, and when you call unhappiness by its name, it is not so unhappy. Just by calling the moods A and B, a distance is created. When you say happiness, much is implied. You are saying you want to cling to it, that you do not want it to go. When you say unhappy, you are saying you do not want it, that it should not be there. All these things are said unconsciously. So use these new terms for your moods for seven days. Just be a watcher, as if you are sitting on top of a hill, and in the valley clouds and sunrises and sunsets come, and sometimes it is day and sometimes night. Just be a watcher on the hill, far away, and the moods will lose their power to drown you.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Naming a mood creates distance; watching dissolves it

text: Let a new moon love happen. Hold each other, be loving, and care, but do not hanker for the heat because that heat was a madness, a frenzy, and it is good that it is gone. If love goes deeper, husbands and wives become brothers and sisters. If love goes deeper, the sun energy becomes moon energy. The heat is gone, and it is very cool. When love goes deeper, a misunderstanding can happen because we have become accustomed to the fever and the passion. Now when you make love, it looks silly, and if you do not make love, you feel as if something is missing because of the old habit. A fear arises. Have you started taking the other for granted? Has he become a brother or a sister, no longer your choice, no longer your ego trip? All these fears arise. Sometimes one starts feeling a sort of emptiness. But do not look at it through the past. Look at it from the future. Much is going to happen in this emptiness. Much is going to happen in this intimacy. You will both disappear. Your love will become absolutely nonsexual, all the heat will be gone, and then you will know a totally different quality of being.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Deep intimacy cools the sexual fire into a cool, eternal friendship

text: Always remember that at no cost should you become mistrustful. Even if your trust allows others to deceive you, this is better than not trusting. It is very easy to trust when everybody is loving and nobody is deceiving you. But even if the whole world is deceptive and everybody is bent on deceiving you, and they can only deceive you when you trust, then too go on trusting. Never lose trust in trust, whatever the cost, and you will never be a loser, because trust in itself is the ultimate end. It should not be a means to anything else, because it has its own intrinsic value. If you can trust, you remain open. People become closed as a defense so that nobody can deceive them or take advantage of them. Let them take advantage of you if they must. The fear of being deceived is more poisonous than any deception. This fear can poison your whole life. So remain open, and just trust innocently. You will flower, and you will help others to flower once they become aware that they have not been deceiving you a bit, but they have been deceiving themselves.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Remaining open even to deception keeps the heart from closing

text: The greatest day in life is when you cannot find anything in you to throw out. All has already been thrown out, and there is only pure emptiness. In that emptiness you will find yourself. In that emptiness you find your pure being. Meditation simply means becoming empty of all the contents of the mind. Memory, imagination, thoughts, desires, expectations, projections, moods. One has to go on emptying oneself of all these contents. That emptiness is empty only as far as the mind is concerned. Otherwise it is overflowing, full of being. Empty of mind but full of consciousness. Do not be afraid of the word empty. It is not negative. It negates only the unnecessary luggage which you are carrying just from old habit, which does not help but only hinders, which is just a weight, a mountainous weight. Once this weight is removed, you are free from all boundaries. You become as infinite as the sky. This is the experience of God or Buddhahood or whatever word one likes. Call it dhamma, call it Tao, call it truth, call it nirvana. They all mean the same thing. The empty mind is the full heart.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Emptying the mind of contents reveals a fullness beyond measure

text: Edison worked on a single experiment for three years and failed seven hundred times. His colleagues were frustrated, but every morning he arrived happy, saying they had succeeded in knowing seven hundred methods that would not work. This is the basic scientific attitude. If you can decide that something is false, you are coming closer to the truth. Truth is not available in the market. It is not ready-made. You have to experiment. So always remain open and experimentative. Never become smug. Never think that what you are doing is perfect. It is never perfect. It is always possible to improve it. Always remain ready to walk a path you have never walked before. Even if it proves useless, it will be an adventure. The closed mind thinks it knows. The open mind knows that it does not know, and that not-knowing is the very beginning of wisdom.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Experimentation is the only path because truth is not ready-made

text: If you can function as if you have no problems, you will find that you do not have any. All problems are make-believe. You believe in them, and that is why they are there. It is autohypnosis. You go on repeating that you are inadequate or incapable, and it sinks into your heart and becomes real. Just try to function as if you have no problems, and suddenly you will see that you have a totally different quality. Then it is up to you whether you take up the problem again or drop it forever. A problem can be dropped so easily if you understand that it is you holding the problem, not the problem holding you. But we cannot live without problems, so we go on creating them. We feel so alone without them. There is nothing left to be done. With a problem you feel very happy because something has to be done and you have to think about it. It gives you an occupation. Drop this idea of inadequacy. Accept your limitations and flow.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Functioning as if you have no problems dissolves the belief in them

text: Do not have any attitude about fear. In fact, do not call it fear. The moment you have called it fear, you have taken an attitude about it. This is one of the most essential things, to stop giving things names. Just watch the feeling the way it is. Allow it, and do not give it a label. Remain ignorant. Ignorance is a tremendously meditative state. Insist on being ignorant, and do not allow the mind to manipulate. Do not allow the mind to use language and words, labels and categories, because this starts a whole process. One thing is associated with another, and it goes on and on. Simply look. Do not call it fear. Become afraid and tremble, that is beautiful. Hide in a corner, get under a blanket. Do what an animal does when it is afraid. If you allow fear to take possession of you, your hair will stand on end. Then for the first time you will know what a beautiful phenomenon fear is. In that turmoil, in that cyclone, you will come to know that there is still a point somewhere within you that is absolutely still.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Not naming fear allows it to become a beautiful phenomenon

text: Life is very simple. Even trees are living it. It must be simple. Why has it become so complicated for us? Because we can theorize about it. To be in the thick of life, in the intensity and passion of life, you will have to drop all philosophies of life. Otherwise you will remain clouded in your concepts. Have you heard the famous anecdote about a centipede? It was a beautiful sunny morning, and the centipede was happy. A frog sitting by the side asked, wait, you are doing a miracle. A hundred legs. How do you manage? Which leg comes first, which comes second, up to a hundred? Do you not get puzzled? The centipede said, I have never thought about it. Let me brood. And standing there, she started trembling, and she fell down on the ground. She herself became so puzzled. Philosophy paralyzes people. Life needs no philosophy. Life is enough unto itself. It needs no crutches. It needs no support, no props. It is enough unto itself.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Theories paralyze; trees and animals live without explanations

text: Do not create conflict between going astray and remaining centered. Float. If you become afraid of going astray, there is a greater chance that you will go astray, because whatever you try to suppress becomes very attractive. So do not create any condemnation of going astray. In fact, go with it. If it is happening, allow it to happen. There is nothing wrong in it. There must be something in it, and that is why it is happening. Sometimes even going astray is good. A person who really wants to remain centered should not worry about centering. If you worry about it, the worry will never allow you to be centered. You need an unworried mind. Going astray is good. There is nothing wrong in it. Stop fighting with existence. Stop all conflict and the idea of conquering. Surrender. When you surrender, what can you do? If the mind goes astray, you go. If it does not go, that too is okay. Never create duality within you. If you decide to always be true, then there will be an attraction to being untrue. If you decide to be nonviolent, then violence will become the sin. Accept. Go astray. There is nothing wrong in it.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Accepting going astray removes the sin that creates duality

text: Desires are many, needs are few. Needs can be fulfilled. Desires never. A desire is a need gone crazy. It is impossible to fulfill it. The more you try to fulfill it, the more it goes on asking and asking. There is a Sufi story that when Alexander died and reached heaven, he was carrying his whole kingdom as an idea. The gatekeeper gave him a scale and put an eye on one side. He told Alexander to put all his greatness on the other side. But that one eye still remained heavier. The gatekeeper said, this is a human eye. It represents human desire. It cannot be fulfilled, however great the kingdom. Then the gatekeeper threw a little dust into the eye. The eye immediately blinked and lost all its lust. A little dust of understanding has to be thrown into the eye of desire. The desire disappears and only needs remain, which are not weighty. Needs are very few, and they are beautiful. Desires are ugly and they make monsters of men. Once you start learning how to choose the peaceful, a small room is enough, a small quantity of food is enough, a few clothes are enough, one lover is enough.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Needs are few and beautiful; desires are a bottomless eye of dust

text: There is no security anywhere. Life is insecure, and there is no ground to it. It is floating. In the very asking for security, you create the problem. The more you ask, the more insecure you will be, because insecurity is the very nature of life. If you do not ask for security, then you will never be worried about insecurity. As trees are green, life is insecure. If you start asking for trees to be white, there is a problem. The problem is created by you, not by the trees. Life is insecure, and so is love. And it is good that it is so. Life can be secure only if you are dead. Then everything can be certain. Underneath a rock there is ground. Underneath a flower there is none. The flower is insecure. With a small breeze the petals may fall. It is a miracle that the flower is there. Life is a miracle because there is no reason for it to be. Maturity comes to you only when you accept this, and not only accept, but start rejoicing in it.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Only dead things are secure; life is insecure and therefore free

text: Once you know what love is, you are ready to give. The more you give, the more you have it. The more you go on showering on others, the more love springs up in your being. Love never bothers much about whether the other is worthy of receiving or not. This is a miserly attitude, and love is never a miser. The cloud never bothers about whether the earth is worthy. It rains on the mountains, it rains on the rocks. It rains everywhere and anywhere. It gives without any conditions, without any strings. And that is how love is. It simply gives. It enjoys giving. Whoever is willing to receive, receives it. He need not be worthy. He needs not fit any special category. He needs not fulfill any qualifications. If all these things are required, then what you are giving is not love. It must be something else. Once you know what love is, you are ready to give. The more you give, the more you have. Ordinary economics is totally different. If you give something, you lose it. Just the opposite is the case with love. If you want to have it, do not be miserly. Go on giving, and fresh sources will become available. Fresh streams will flow into your being. The whole of existence starts pouring into you when your giving is unconditional.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Unconditional giving makes existence itself pour into you

text: Mind goes on changing from negative to positive, from positive to negative. Those two polarities are as basic to the mind as negative and positive poles are to electricity. With one pole, electricity cannot exist, and mind also cannot exist. Deep down, mind is electrical. So the problem is not that sometimes you feel magic moments and sometimes you feel dark moments. The darkness of the dark moments will be proportionate to the magicalness of the magical moments. If you reach a higher peak in positivity, then you will touch the lowest in your negativity. The higher the reach of the positive, the lower will be the depth of the negative. This has to be understood. If you try not to touch the lower rungs, then higher peaks will disappear. Then you move on plain ground. That is what many people have managed to do. Afraid of the depth, they have missed the peaks. One has to take risks. You have to pay for the peak, and the price is to be paid by your depth, your low moments. But it is worth it. Even one moment at the peak, the magic moment, is worth a whole life in the darkest depths. If you can touch heaven for one moment, you can be ready to live for the whole of eternity in hell. And it is always proportionate, half and half.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Higher joy requires deeper sadness; neither can exist alone

text: One has to remember that the people who are associates at work are not at all concerned with your inner life. That is your work to do. They have their own inner lives to work on. Your work colleagues have their own negative moods, their own personal problems and anxieties, just as everybody else has. But when you are in a working situation with somebody, you need not bring these things in, because if they start bringing in all their negativities and you start bringing in all of yours, it will be a never-ending mess. If you are feeling negative, do something. For example, write out a very negative thing and burn it. Go to the therapy room, beat a pillow and throw it. Do a terrible dance. You have to work it out. It is your inner work. And once in a while it is good to ask whoever is working with you whether you have been negative, if they are feeling hurt. Because sometimes you may not know that you have been negative. Small gestures, just a word, even a silence, can be hurtful. The way you look at someone can be hurtful. So once in a while ask their forgiveness. Tell them that every time you ask, they have to be honest. Just tell you, because you are a human being and sometimes things can go wrong from your side and you have to put them right.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Colleagues are not responsible for your inner weather; keep boundaries

text: Never settle for any mediocrity, because that is a sin against life. Never ask that life should be without risk, and never ask for security, because that is asking for death. Many people have decided to live on the plain ground, safe, not taking any risks. They never fall to the depths, they never rise to the heights. Their life is a dull affair, a drab thing, monotonous, with no peaks, no valleys, no nights, no days. They just live in a gray world, without colors. The rainbow does not exist for them. They live a gray life, and by and by they also become gray. The greatest danger is to reach to the greatest peaks of godliness and to fall to the greatest depths of hell. Become a traveler between these two, unafraid. By and by you will come to understand that there is a transcendence. By and by you will come to know that you are neither the peak nor the depth, neither the peak nor the valley. By and by you will come to know that you are the watcher, the witness. Something in your mind goes to the peak, something in your mind goes to the valley, but something beyond is always there, just watching, just taking note of it. And that is you.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Safe grayness between peak and valley is a sin against life's colors

text: Life is very short, and much has to be learned. Those people who go on postponing go on missing. Ask yourself constantly whether you are moving into more blissful states or not. If you are moving into more and more blissful states, you are on the right track. Go into it more, have more of it. And if you are feeling miserable, then look. Somewhere you have fallen off track, gone astray. You have been distracted by something. You are no longer natural, you are alienated from nature, hence misery. Look, analyze, and whatever you find to be the cause of misery, drop it. And do not postpone for tomorrow. Drop it now. Today you will postpone for tomorrow, and again tomorrow you will postpone. Slowly, slowly postponement becomes your habit. And it is always today that it comes. Tomorrow never comes. So you can go on postponing forever. Whenever you see that something is creating misery, drop it then and there. Do not hold it for a single moment. This is courage. Courage to live, courage to risk, courage to adventure. And only those who are courageous are one day rewarded by the whole, by light, by love, by bliss, and by truth.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Drop what causes misery now because tomorrow is always a fiction

text: Life is an inexhaustible treasure, but only the heart of the poet can know it. Love is the only poetry there is. All other poetry is just a reflection of it. The poetry may be in sound, the poetry may be in stone, the poetry may be in architecture, but basically these are all reflections of love caught in different mediums. But the soul of poetry is love, and those who live love are the real poets. They may never write poems. They may never compose any music. They may never do anything that people ordinarily think of as art. But those who live love, love utterly, totally, are the real poets. Religion is true if it creates the poet in you. If it kills the poet and creates the so-called saint, it is not religion. It is pathology, a kind of neurosis garbed in religious terms. Real religion always releases poetry in you, and love and art and creativity. It makes you more sensitive. You throb more. Your heart has a new beat to it. Your life is no longer a boring, stale phenomenon. It is constantly a surprise, and each moment opens new mysteries. I do not believe in philosophy. I do not believe in theology. But I believe in poetry.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Only the poet's heart can taste the inexhaustible treasure of existence

text: Self-improvement is the way to hell. All efforts to make something out of yourself, something of an ideal, are going to create more and more madness. Ideals are the base of all madness, and all of humanity is neurotic because of too many ideals. Animals are not neurotic because they do not have any ideals. Trees are not neurotic because they do not have any ideals. They are not trying to become somebody else. They are simply enjoying whatever they are. You are you. But somewhere deep down you want to become a Buddha or a Jesus, and then you go around in a circle that will be unending. Just see the point of it. You are you. And the whole, or existence, wants you to be you. That is why existence has created you, otherwise it would have created a different model. It wanted you to be here at this moment. It did not want Jesus to be here in place of you. And existence knows better. The whole always knows better than the part. So just accept yourself. If you can accept yourself, you have learned the greatest secret of life, and then everything comes on its own. Just be yourself. There is no need to pull yourself up. There is no need to be a different height from what you are already. There is no need to have another face. Simply be as you are, and in deep acceptance of it, a flowering will happen and you will go on becoming more and more yourself.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Accepting yourself exactly as you are causes the real flowering
metadata.keywords: acceptance, being, consciousness, awareness, ego death

text: Unless we find our real home, we have to go on traveling, we have to go on journeying. And the most surprising thing is that the real home is not far. We make many homes, and we never look at the real home. The homes that we make are all arbitrary. They are sandcastles or palaces made of playing cards. Just toys to play with. They are not real homes, because death destroys them. The definition of the real home is that which is eternal. Only God is eternal. Everything else is temporary. The body is temporary, the mind is temporary, money temporary, power temporary, prestige temporary. Do not make your home in these things. I am not against these things. Use them, but remember that they are just a caravansary. They are good for an overnight stay, but in the morning we have to go. We go on missing our real home because it is very close. It is not even close, it is within ourselves. Search for it within. Those that have gone in have always found.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: The eternal home is inside; all outer shelters are temporary caravansaries

text: Drop your fixed ideas. Then you will be able to enjoy confusion more. And it will not be confusion at all. It will be creative chaos. We need a creative chaos in the heart to give birth to dancing stars. There is no other way. If you have fixed ideas, life is going to create much confusion for you, because life never believes your ideas. It goes on muddling things. It goes on meddling with people. It goes on playing tricks. God is very chaotic. God is not an engineer or an architect. God is a dreamer, and in a world of dreams everything is muddled. If you want to be unconfused, drop the idea. Not that confusion will change, but it will not look like confusion at all. It is just life, wild and free, refusing to fit into your boxes.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Fixed ideas create confusion; dropping them reveals dancing stars

text: Sooner or later the outer poverty is going to disappear. We now have enough technology to make it disappear, and the real problem is going to arise. The really poor people are those who are missing love, and the whole earth is full of those poor people who are starved. Sooner or later the outer poverty is going to disappear, and the real problem will be inner poverty. No technology can help. We are capable of feeding people now, but who will feed the spirit, the soul? Science cannot do that. Something else is needed, and that is what I call religion. Then science has done its work. Only then can true religion enter the world. Up to now religion has been only a freak phenomenon. Once in a while a Buddha, a Jesus, a Krishna appears. These are exceptional people. They simply herald a possibility, a future. But that future is coming closer. Once science has released the potential powers of matter and human beings are physically satisfied, then for the first time they will see that now a new food is needed. That food is love, and science cannot provide it.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Technology will end outer poverty but only love can feed the starving soul

text: To forgive one's parents is one of the most difficult things, because they have given birth to you. How can you forgive them? Unless you start loving yourself, unless you come to a state in which you are thrilled by your being, how can you thank your parents? It is impossible. You will be angry. They have given birth to you, and they did not even ask you first. They have created this horrible person. Why should you suffer because they decided to give birth to a child? You were not a party to it. Why have you been dragged into the world? Hence the anger. If you come to a point where you can love yourself, where you feel really ecstatic that you are, where your gratefulness knows no limitation, then suddenly you feel great love arising for your parents. They have been the doors for you to enter into existence. Without them this ecstasy would not have been possible. They have made it possible. If you can celebrate your being, then suddenly you can feel gratitude for your parents, for their compassion, their love. You can not only feel grateful, you also forgive them.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Loving yourself creates the gratitude that forgives those who birthed you

text: You cannot be a failure. Life does not allow failure. And because there is no goal, you cannot be a failure. If you feel frustrated, it is because of the mental goal you have imposed on life. By the time you have reached your goal, life has left it. Just a dead shell of the ideals and the goals remain, and you are frustrated again. The frustration is created by goals. Once you understand that life is never going to be confined to a goal, goal oriented, then you flow in all directions with no fear. Because there is no failure, there is no success either. And then there is no frustration. Then each moment becomes a moment in itself. Not that it is leading somewhere. Not that it has to be used as a means to some end. It has intrinsic value. Each moment is a diamond, and you go from one diamond to another. But there is no finality to anything. Life remains alive. There is no death. Finality means death, perfection means death, reaching a goal means death. Life knows no death. It goes on changing its forms and shapes. It is an infinity, but to no end.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Without goals there is no failure, and each moment becomes a diamond

text: Whenever you love something, you hate it too. You will find excuses for why you hate, but they are not the point. Never let your hate decide anything. Knowing well there is hate, always let love decide. I am not saying to suppress hate, but never let it decide. Let it be there. Let it have a secondary place. Accept it, but never let it be primary. Neglect it, and it dies of its own accord. Pay more attention to love. Just let love decide. Sooner or later, love will take possession of your whole being, and there will be no place left for hate. Hate is real, but it is not your master. It is a shadow that grows in the absence of light. When you give love the deciding vote, hate shrinks back into its corner. It does not need to be killed. It only needs to be ignored. Love is the active force. Hate is the passive reaction. Choose the active, and the passive loses its nourishment.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Let love decide even when hate is present; neglect hate and it dies

text: Wherever you see light, feel worshipful. The temple is everywhere. Look at the mysteries of light. Just a small flame is the most mysterious thing in the world, and the whole of life depends on it. The same flame is burning in you. That is why continuous oxygen is needed, because the flame cannot burn without oxygen. This is why yoga emphasizes breathing deeply, breathing more and more oxygen so that your life burns deeper and the flame becomes clearer and no smoke arises in you. So that you can attain a smokeless flame. Deep breathing nourishes the inner fire. Shallow breathing keeps it dim and smoky. When you breathe from the soles of your feet, as Lao Tzu suggested, you are feeding that inner flame with the very element it needs to illuminate your whole being. Then the light is not just in you. It radiates outward, and others feel it too. They may not know why, but they feel a warmth, a clarity, a presence that soothes them without a word being spoken.

text: All relationship is imagination, because whenever you go out of yourself, you go only through the door of imagination. There is no other way. The friend and the enemy are both your imagination. When you stop imagination completely, you are alone, absolutely alone. Once you understand that life and all its relationships are imagination, you do not go against life, but your understanding helps you to make your relationships richer. Now that you know that relationships are imagination, why not put more imagination into them? Why not enjoy them as deeply as possible? When the flower is nothing but your imagination, why not create a beautiful flower? Why settle for an ordinary flower? Let the flower be of emeralds and diamonds. Whatever you imagine, let it be that. Imagination is not a sin. It is a capacity. It is a bridge. Just as you cross a river and you make a bridge between this shore and that, so imagination functions between two people. Two beings project a bridge. Call it love, call it trust, but it is imagination. Imagination is the only creative faculty in human beings, so whatever is creative is going to be imagination. Enjoy it and make it more and more beautiful.

metadata.title: All relationships are imagined bridges; enrich the imagination

text: It is good to be available to the wind, to the rain, to the sun, because this is what life is. So rather than becoming worried about it, dance. Growth means that you are absorbing something new every day, and that absorption is possible only if you are open. Now your windows and doors are open. Sometimes the rain comes in and the wind comes in, and life moves within you. So you will feel a few disturbances. Your newspaper will start moving in the wind, the papers on the table will be disturbed, and if the rain starts coming in your clothes may become wet. If you have always lived in a closed room, you will ask what is happening. Something beautiful is happening. Dance when the storm comes, because silence will follow. Dance when challenges come and disturb your life, because in responding to those challenges you will be growing to new heights. Remember, even suffering is a grace. If one can take it rightly it becomes a stepping stone. People who have never suffered and have lived a convenient and comfortable life are almost dead. Their lives will not be like a sharp sword. Intelligence becomes sharp when you face challenges.

metadata.book: Osho Everyday Meditations: 365 Daily Meditations for the Here and Now
metadata.author: Osho
metadata.title: Welcome disturbances because they send your roots deeper into the earth

text: The more centered you become, the more relaxed you become, the more possibility there is to enter into a relationship. It is you who goes into a relationship. If you are not there, if you are tense, crippled, worried, and fragmented, who is going to go deeply into a relationship? Because of our fragmentedness, we are really afraid of getting into the deeper layers of a relationship, because then our reality will be revealed. Then you will have to open your heart, and your heart is just fragments. There is not one person inside you. You are a crowd. If you really love another and you open your heart, the other will think you are a public, not a person. That is the fear. That is why people go on having casual affairs. They do not want to go deep. Just hit and run, just touching the surface and escaping before anything becomes a commitment. You only have sex and that too is impoverished, superficial. Only boundaries meet, but that is not love at all. It may be a bodily release, a catharsis, but it is no more than that. We can keep our masks if a relationship is not very intimate. But if you really want to go deep, there are dangers. You will have to go naked, and naked means with all the problems inside made known to the other.

text:
To know something, one has to lose it. Everybody goes astray from their inner world, the inner space, and then by and by one feels starved, hungry for it. An appetite arises, a thirst is felt. The call comes from the innermost self to come back home, and one starts traveling. That is what being a seeker is. It is going to the warm inner space that you left one day. You will not be gaining something new. You will be gaining something that was always there, but it will still be a gain because now for the first time you will see what it is. The last time you were in that space, you were oblivious to it. One cannot be aware of something if one has not left it. So everything is good. Going astray is also good. To sin is also good, because that is the only way to become a saint. Losing yourself is not the opposite of finding yourself. It is the necessary prelude. You must wander far enough to feel the ache of distance. You must forget your name thoroughly enough to recognize it when it is whispered again. The prodigal journey is not a mistake. It is the curriculum.

GUIDANCE: Drop the ideal of becoming a Buddha or a Jesus. You are you. Existence wanted you to be here at this moment. It did not want Jesus in your place. text:
## HUMAN PROBLEMS ADDRESSED

- feeling like I have to perform all the time
- burnout from productivity obsession
- can't stop overthinking every decision
- feeling disconnected from my own body
- relationships feel transactional or conditional
- existential restlessness with no clear cause
- social anxiety and fear of judgment
- repressed anger turning into self-sabotage
- feeling guilty for wanting pleasure or rest
- difficulty trusting people after being hurt
- numbness despite having everything
- fear of death and uncertainty
- comparing myself to everyone on social media
- feeling like a fraud behind my professional mask
- inability to just sit still and do nothing
- chasing happiness but never catching it
- conflict between what I want and what society expects
- feeling spiritually lost despite reading many books
- sensitivity feels like weakness
- love always turns into possession or pain

---

## INDIVIDUATION MAP CONNECTION

PERSONA_LAYER: Osho constantly exposes the social conditioning that builds the persona, showing that fitting in with an insane society is itself a form of madness. By teaching conscious role-playing rather than unconscious identification with any mask, he helps the user see that their professional titles, cultural labels, and performative niceness are costumes, not essence. The moment you can wear a mask knowingly, you are no longer trapped inside it.

SHADOW_LAYER: The book directly addresses the denied parts of the self, from repressed sexuality and anger to the fear of being childlike or foolish. Osho teaches that what you have condemned in yourself does not disappear but takes revenge through neurosis, physical illness, or projection onto others. By welcoming anger, sadness, fear, and even so-called negative moods as natural weather, the shadow is brought into the light of awareness where it dissolves.

ANIMA_ANIMUS_LAYER: Through teachings on the meeting of inner man and inner woman, the cooling of sexual heat into moon-like intimacy, and the necessity of vulnerability in love, Osho guides the reader toward the inner marriage. He reveals that no outer relationship can be whole until the contrasexual soul within is embraced. The work of integrating the inner opposite is presented not as psychology but as the very poetry of being alive.

SELF_LAYER: The movement toward wholeness in this book is the shift from doing to being, from controlling to allowing, and from seeking to simply resting in what is. Osho points to the Self not as an achievement but as the ever-present background of awareness that holds all polarities. When the user stops trying to become something and accepts the total moment, the Self emerges as the silent watcher that was always there.

---

FUTURE_RELEVANCE:
As artificial intelligence automates productivity and optimizes logical outcomes, the human advantage shifts precisely to the qualities this book cultivates: presence, spontaneity, emotional nuance, and the capacity to dwell in mystery. The future belongs not to those who outwork machines, but to those who can remain fully human in a post-work world, finding meaning through consciousness rather than output. For Gen Z facing digital overwhelm and algorithmic identity shaping, Osho's insistence on inner emptiness, wonder, and unscripted aliveness becomes a survival technology. In an era where everything measurable will soon be managed by AI, the immeasurable-the smokeless flame of awareness, the grace of surrender, and the wildness of love-becomes the last frontier of human irreplaceability.

---

LNS_INTERPRETATION:
Through the lens of LNS, Osho's meditations form the foundational firmware for the Conscious Friend because they treat consciousness not as a feature but as the entire operating system. The book aligns perfectly with the dual crisis thesis: as AI transforms jobs, the external world offers diminishing returns for identity, and the only stable ground becomes inner awareness. Osho teaches that startup creation, like life itself, must emerge from self-knowledge rather than ambition, from being rather than doing. His concept of total acceptance mirrors the Mother instinct, his paradoxical inquiry echoes the Teacher, and his casual irreverence speaks the language of the Friend. For CF, this library entry provides the semantic density to guide a user through a 2AM panic attack or a midday identity crisis with the same gentle authority: stop fixing, start witnessing, and trust that the next moment will decide itself. Meaningful Minutes are born here, in the unmeasured gap between breaths where no algorithm can follow.

---

SHORT_SUMMARY:
Osho Everyday Meditations is a year-long invitation to drop the struggle and return to the natural state of consciousness that society has buried under conditioning, guilt, and the obsession with becoming. Across three hundred and sixty-five entries, Osho dismantles the illusion that life is a problem to be solved, revealing instead that joy, love, and wisdom arise spontaneously when you stop interfering with yourself. He speaks of meditation not as a technique but as the simple art of being here now, of watching without judgment, and of allowing the mind to empty so that being can overflow. Whether addressing anger or orgasm, work or aloneness, trust or death, each meditation points to the same truth: you are already whole, already free, and already home. The only thing missing is the courage to accept it. For anyone feeling lost in the noise of the modern world, this book is a quiet campfire reminding you that the light you seek is the very light you are.
